```erlang
-module(vowel_rearranger).
-export([solve/1]).

%% @doc
%% Reorders only the vowels in a string s based on frequency and first appearance.
%% Frequency order: Non-increasing.
%% Tie-breaker: Order of first appearance (increasing).
%% @spec solve(string()) -> string()
solve(S) :-
    % 1. Identify vowels and record necessary statistics
    findall({Index, Char}, 
            (nth1(Index, S, Char), is_vowel(Char)), 
            VowelLocations),

    % Calculate frequencies and first occurrences
    calculate_stats(VowelLocations, Frequencies, FirstOccurrences),

    % Extract unique vowels present in S
    get_unique_vowels(VowelLocations, UniqueVowels),

    % 2. Sort the unique vowels based on criteria
    sort_vowels(UniqueVowels, Frequencies, FirstOccurrences, SortedVowels),

    % 3. Construct the sequence of sorted vowels (respecting counts)
    build_sorted_sequence(SortedVowels, Frequencies, SortedVowelSequence),

    % 4. Reconstruct the string
    reconstruct_string(S, VowelLocations, SortedVowelSequence, ResultString).

%% --- Helper Functions ---

is_vowel('a') -> true;
is_vowel('e') -> true;
is_vowel('i') -> true;
is_vowel('o') -> true;
is_vowel('u') -> true;
is_vowel(_) -> false.

%% Calculates frequency map and first occurrence map from vowel locations
calculate_stats(VowelLocations, Frequencies, FirstOccurrences) ->
    aggregate_map(VowelLocations,
                   fun(Idx, Char) -> {Char, Idx} end,
                   fun(Map, {Char, Idx}) ->
                       update_map(Map, Char, fun(Count) -> Count + 1 end, 0), % Frequency
                       update_map(Map, Char, fun(_Idx) -> Idx end, undefined)  % First Occurrence
                   end,
                   [],
                   Frequencies_Raw),
    
    % Separate frequencies and first occurrences into final maps
    extract_maps(Frequencies_Raw, Frequencies, FirstOccurrences).

% Helper to update a map (like map.update in JS)
update_map(Map, Key, UpdateFn, Initial) ->
    case maps:get(Map, Key) of
        _ -> maps:put(Map, Key, Initial)
    ;
        Value -> maps:put(Map, Key, UpdateFn(Value))
    end.

% Structure: MapKey -> {Frequency, FirstOccurrence}
extract_maps(StatsMap, Frequencies, FirstOccurrences) ->
    % We need to process StatsMap to get two separate maps
    maps:fold(fun(Key, {Freq, FirstOcc}, Acc) ->
        lists:replace(Acc, Key, {Freq, FirstOcc})
    end,
    [],
    StatsMap,
    ListStats),
    
    Frequencies = maps:from_list(ListStats, fun({Key, {Freq, _}}) -> {Key, Freq} end),
    FirstOccurrences = maps:from_list(ListStats, fun({Key, {_Freq, FirstOcc}}) -> {Key, FirstOcc} end).


% Extracts unique vowels present in the string
get_unique_vowels(VowelLocations, UniqueVowels) ->
    lists:foldl(fun({_, Char}, Acc) ->
        case member(Char, Acc) of
            true -> Acc
            false -> [Char | Acc]
        end
    end, [], VowelLocations).


% Sorts unique vowels based on criteria
sort_vowels(UniqueVowels, Frequencies, FirstOccurrences, SortedVowels) ->
    lists:sort(fun(V1, V2) ->
        % Criteria:
        % 1. Frequency (Descending)
        % 2. First Appearance Index (Ascending)
        
        Freq1 = maps:get(Frequencies, V1, 0),
        Freq2 = maps:get(Frequencies, V2, 0),
        
        if Freq1 <> Freq2 ->
            % Descending frequency: Freq2 - Freq1 (if we treat them as numbers)
            if Freq1 > Freq2 -> -1 ; Freq1 < Freq2 -> 1 ; 0 end
        else
            % Frequencies equal: Ascending first occurrence index
            First1 = maps:get(FirstOccurrences, V1, 0),
            First2 = maps:get(FirstOccurrences, V2, 0),
            if First1 < First2 -> -1 ; First1 > First2 -> 1 ; 0 end
        end
    end, UniqueVowels).


% Builds the final sequence of vowels by repeating each according to its frequency
build_sorted_sequence(SortedVowels, Frequencies, SortedVowelSequence) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Vowel, Acc) ->
        Freq = maps:get(Frequencies, Vowel, 0),
        List = lists:replicate(Freq, Vowel),
        [List | Acc]
    end, [], SortedVowels)
    |> lists:flatten().

% Reconstructs the final string by placing sorted vowels back into original vowel positions
reconstruct_string(S, VowelLocations, SortedVowelSequence, ResultString) ->
    % Initialize result as a list of characters from S
    InitialResult = lists:seq(S),
    
    % Fold over the vowel locations and sequentially replace characters
    lists:foldl(fun({Index, _}, CurrentResult, VowelChar) ->
        % Index here is 1-based position from nth1 used in VowelLocations
        lists:replace(CurrentResult, Index, VowelChar)
    end,
    InitialResult,
    VowelLocations,
    SortedVowelSequence)
    |> lists:to_binary(). % Convert list of chars back to binary/string

%% @doc
%% Reorders only the vowels in a string based on frequency (non-increasing)
%% and first appearance order (for tie-breaking).
%%
%% @spec solve(list(list(int))) -> list(list(int))
solve(S) ->
    % Convert the list of chars (or list of lists of ints if we treat input as list of chars)
    % Since Erlang strings are usually lists of chars, we'll work with lists.
    case map_to_list(S) of
        VowelsAndConsonants ->
            % 1. Identify vowels and store their original indices and characters
            [vowel_indices, char_map, first_occurrence_map] = collect_vowel_info(VowelsAndConsonants);

            % 2. Calculate frequencies
            frequencies = calculate_frequencies(char_map),

            % 3. Get unique vowels present
            unique_vowels = get_unique_vowels(char_map),

            % 4. Sort unique vowels based on criteria
            SortedUniqueVowels = sort_vowels(unique_vowels, frequencies, first_occurrence_map),

            % 5. Construct the final sorted sequence of all vowels
            SortedVowelSequence = build_sorted_sequence(SortedUniqueVowels, frequencies),

            % 6. Reconstruct the string
            FinalList = substitute_vowels(VowelsAndConsonants, vowel_indices, SortedVowelSequence),
            
            lists:to_binary(FinalList)
    end.

%% Helper to convert input list of chars to a standard list of characters
map_to_list(<<"...">>List) ->
    [C || C <- List];
map_to_list(L) ->
    L.

%% Helper to check if a character is a vowel
is_vowel(C) ->
    case C of
        'a' -> true;
        'e' -> true;
        'i' -> true;
        'o' -> true;
        'u' -> true;
        _ -> false
    end.

%% Collects indices, counts (char_map), and first appearance positions
collect_vowel_info(S) ->
    {VowelIndices, CharMap, FirstOccurrenceMap} = 
        lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Char) ->
            case Acc of
                {VIs, CM, FO} ->
                    case is_vowel(Char) of
                        true ->
                            % Update indices
                            NewVIs = [length(VIs) | VIs],
                            
                            % Update counts (CharMap)
                            NewCM = case maps:get(Char, CM) of
                                0 -> maps:put(Char, 1, CM);
                                Count -> maps:put(Char, Count + 1, CM)
                            end,
                            
                            % Update first occurrence (FirstOccurrenceMap)
                            NewFO = case maps:get(Char, FO) of
                                undefined -> maps:put(Char, length(VIs), FO); % Use current index length(VIs) as first index
                                _ -> FO
                            end,
                            
                            {NewVIs, NewCM, NewFO};
                        false -> {VIs, CM, FO}
                    end
        end, {[], maps:new(), maps:new()}, S);
    
    % VowelIndices were collected in reverse order of appearance, reverse them back
    {reverse(VowelIndices), CharMap, FirstOccurrenceMap}.

%% Calculates frequencies (though CharMap already holds this)
calculate_frequencies(CharMap) ->
    CharMap.

%% Gets unique vowels present in the string
get_unique_vowels(CharMap) ->
    maps:keys(CharMap).

%% Sorts unique vowels based on frequency (desc) then first appearance (asc)
sort_vowels(UniqueVowels, Frequencies, FirstOccurrenceMap) ->
    lists:sort(fun(V1, V2) ->
        case {maps:get(V1, Frequencies), maps:get(V2, Frequencies)} of
            {F1, F2} when F1 =:= F2 ->
                % Frequencies are equal, compare first appearance (Ascending index)
                case {maps:get(V1, FirstOccurrenceMap), maps:get(V2, FirstOccurrenceMap)} of
                    {First1, First2} ->
                        if First1 < First2 -> -1;
                        else if First1 > First2 -> 1;
                        else -> 0
                        end;
                    _ -> 0 % Should not happen if vowel is in CharMap
                end;
            {F1, _} when F1 > F2 -> -1; % V1 has higher frequency -> V1 comes first (negative)
            {_, F2} when F1 < F2 -> 1;  % V2 has higher frequency -> V2 comes first (positive)
            _ -> 0
        end
    end, UniqueVowels).

%% Builds the final ordered sequence of all vowels
build_sorted_sequence(SortedUniqueVowels, Frequencies) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Seq, Vowel) ->
        case maps:get(Vowel, Frequencies) of
            Count -> 
                lists:duplicate(Vowel, Count) ++ Seq;
            _ -> Seq
        end
    end, [], SortedUniqueVowels).

%% Substitutes the original vowels with the new sorted sequence
substitute_vowels(S, VowelIndices, SortedVowelSequence) ->
    % Create a mutable list representation of S
    ListS = lists:map(fun(C) -> case C of {C, _} -> C; _ -> C end, S),

    NewList = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, {Index, Vowel}) ->
        lists:replace(Acc, Index, Vowel)
    end, ListS, lists:zip(VowelIndices, SortedVowelSequence)),
    
    NewList.

    lists:sort(fun(V1, V2) ->
        case {maps:get(V1, Frequencies), maps:get(V2, Frequencies)} of

%% @doc
%% Reorders the vowels in a string based on frequency (non-increasing) 
%% and then by first appearance (increasing) for ties.
%% @spec solve(string()) -> string()
solve(String) ->
    Vowels = <<"aeiou">>,
    VowelChars = [<<"a">>, <<"e">>, <<"i">>, <<"o">>, <<"u">>],

    UniqueVowels = get_unique_vowels(Counts, FirstOccurrence),

    SortedVowelSequence = build_sorted_sequence(SortedVowelOrder, Counts),
    
    %% Step 4: Reconstruct the string
    ResultList = substitute_vowels(String, VowelIndices, SortedVowelSequence),
    
    list_to_string(ResultList).

%% Helper function to record counts, first occurrence, and vowel positions
record_vowel_info(String, VowelChars) ->
    StringLength = string_length(String),
    
    Counts = #{},
    FirstOccurrence = #{},
    VowelIndices = [],
    
    {Counts, FirstOccurrence, VowelIndices} = 
        lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Index) ->
            Case = string_at(String, Index),
            
            if lists:member(Case, VowelChars) ->
                OldCounts = maps:get(Acc, #{}, []),
                OldFirst = maps:get(Acc, #{}, []),
                OldIndices = maps:get(Acc, [], []),
                
                NewCounts = maps:update(Acc, Case, fun(_ -> maps:get(OldCounts, Case, 0) + 1 end), OldCounts),
                NewFirst = case maps:get(OldFirst, Case) of
                    undefined -> maps:put(OldFirst, Case, Index);
                    _ -> OldFirst
                end,
                NewIndices = maps:put(Acc, [Index | OldIndices], OldIndices),
                
                {NewCounts, NewFirst, [Index | OldIndices]}
            else
                Acc
            end,
        {Counts, FirstOccurrence, VowelIndices}, 0),
    
    {Counts, FirstOccurrence, VowelIndices}.

%% Helper function to get unique vowels present in the string
get_unique_vowels(Counts, _FirstOccurrence) ->
    maps:keys(Counts).

%% Helper function to sort the unique vowels
sort_vowels(UniqueVowels, Counts, FirstOccurrence) ->
    lists:sort(fun(V1, V2) ->
        Freq1 = maps:get(Counts, V1, 0),
        Freq2 = maps:get(Counts, V2, 0),
        
        CaseFreq = 
            if Freq1 <> Freq2 ->
                % Decreasing frequency: Freq2 - Freq1
                if Freq1 < Freq2 -> -1 ; Freq1 > Freq2 -> 1 ; 0
            else
                % Frequencies equal: compare first appearance index (Ascending)
                First1 = maps:get(FirstOccurrence, V1, -1),
                First2 = maps:get(FirstOccurrence, V2, -1),
                
                if First1 < First2 -> -1 ; First1 > First2 -> 1 ; 0

        in

build_sorted_sequence(SortedVowelOrder, Counts) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Vowel) ->

    end, [], SortedVowelOrder).

%% Utility: converts list of binary strings to one binary string

%% Utility: converts string to list of binary strings

    string_chars(String),
    lists:map(fun(Char) -> <<"">>[Char] end, string_chars(String)).

%% Utility: inserts an element at a specific index in a list
lists:insert_at(List, Index, Element) ->
    case Index of
        0 -> [Element | List];
        _ -> lists:insert_at(tail(List), Index - 1, Element)
    end.

%% Utility: tail of a list (all elements except the first)
tail([]) -> [];
tail([_|Tail]) -> Tail.

%% Utility: zip two lists together
zip(List1, List2) ->

        {[_|T1], [_|T2]} -> {{_ | T1}, {_ | T2}} :: {{elem, rest}} ;
        {[], _} -> [] ;
        {_, []} -> []
    end.
